about

Infrastructure that ships itself

I'm a DevOps Engineer with hands-on experience automating AWS infrastructure, running CI/CD pipelines, and operating Kubernetes platforms in client-facing production environments.

At Adaequare, I worked across two engagements — building Terraform + Jenkins pipelines for a Java application platform, and supporting an AWS EKS cluster's observability, networking, and storage layers. I also shipped a Node.js app across AWS and GCP to get comfortable thinking multi-cloud.

$ git log --oneline experience

Deploy history

Adaequare Info Pvt Ltd · DevOps Engineer (Graduate Engineer Trainee) · Feb 2024 – Oct 2025

STAGE 01 · INFRASTRUCTURE & CI/CD
Infrastructure Automation & CI/CD — AWS
Client: Cotality
  • Automated AWS infrastructure provisioning with Terraform modules (VPC, EC2, Security Groups, S3), improving infrastructure consistency by 35%.
  • Designed and supported Jenkins CI/CD pipelines for Java applications using Maven, Nexus artifact management, and automated EC2 deployments.
  • Integrated Terraform + Ansible inside Jenkins pipelines for end-to-end provisioning and configuration, cutting setup time by ~40%.
  • Built Docker-based Jenkins agents with Terraform, Ansible, and AWS CLI for isolated, repeatable pipeline runs.
  • Troubleshot Terraform validation errors, IAM permission issues, and Jenkins failures to keep releases stable.
STAGE 02 · PLATFORM & OBSERVABILITY
AWS EKS Platform Support & Observability
Client: CODA
  • Managed AWS EKS workloads — pod troubleshooting, health checks, log analysis, and cluster validation.
  • Configured Kubernetes Ingress, LoadBalancer services, and TLS automation with cert-manager and Let's Encrypt.
  • Deployed an observability stack — Prometheus, Grafana, Loki, Promtail, Node Exporter, cAdvisor — for metrics and centralized logging.
  • Configured IRSA and the AWS EBS CSI Driver for secure pod-level access and dynamic volume provisioning.
STAGE 03 · MULTI-CLOUD
Multi-Cloud Application Deployment
AWS + GCP
  • Deployed a Node.js application across AWS and GCP using Terraform and GitLab CI/CD.
  • Automated infrastructure provisioning and application deployment across multi-cloud environments.
$ ls -la projects/

Engagements, packaged

The same three engagements above, as standalone builds. Full write-ups below.

INFRA / CI-CD
Terraform + Jenkins Pipeline

End-to-end Java app delivery: Terraform-provisioned AWS infra, Jenkins + Maven + Nexus pipelines, Ansible config, and Dockerized build agents.

TerraformJenkinsAnsibleAWS
PLATFORM / K8S
EKS Observability Stack

Production EKS support with Ingress, TLS automation, and a full Prometheus/Grafana/Loki monitoring stack backed by IRSA + EBS CSI storage.

EKSPrometheusGrafanacert-manager
MULTI-CLOUD
Cross-Cloud Node.js Deploy

Same Node.js application deployed in parallel to AWS and GCP, provisioned with Terraform and shipped through GitLab CI/CD.

AWSGCPGitLab CITerraform
$ cat case-studies/*.md

Deep dives & write-ups

Documented builds — full architecture, reasoning, and the gotchas — written up end to end.

AWS + GCP
Multi-Cloud DevOps Architecture

Mirrored AWS (ALB → ECS Fargate) and GCP (Load Balancer → Managed Instance Groups) stacks behind a shared /api/* routing pattern, with scheduled rolling restarts and auto-healing on the GCP side.

Read the doc ↗
EKS · SECURITY
Secure Monitoring Stack on Amazon EKS

TLS-secured ingress in front of the app, EBS CSI gp3-backed persistent storage, Prometheus + Grafana for metrics, Loki + Promtail for logs, and Kyverno policies for namespace protection.

Read on Medium ↗
CI/CD · TERRAFORM
Zero-Touch AWS Deployment

A git push triggers a GitLab pipeline — validate, provision, deploy, test, cleanup — that runs Terraform against a locked-down VPC and ships a Dockerized app to EC2 with no manual steps.

Read the doc ↗
GCP · DATA
Streaming Pipeline: Pub/Sub → Dataflow → BigQuery

Two paths into the same warehouse — a batch job reading CSVs from Cloud Storage, and a streaming job consuming live events off Pub/Sub — both transformed by Dataflow and landed in BigQuery.

Read on Medium ↗
GCP · ORCHESTRATION
Apache Airflow on GCP, from scratch

Terraform-built GCP infra (VPC, GCE VM, Cloud SQL, GCS, service accounts) bootstrapped by a startup script, running Airflow as a Docker Compose stack with DAGs synced continuously from a GCS bucket.
